purpose: Full-split gate for the B49 QR-free Gram-Schmidt candidate (candidate_claude.py @ f54b23b), to make it fully submission-ready. Step 7 requires the candidate's OWN Full paired gate; B46's B47 gate does not transfer since GS is a different estimator (Gram-Schmidt frame vs qr frame). Also rigorously confirms GS matches B46 on the Full split.
method: Ran the GS candidate on the complete 1000-MLP Full split via B26's chunked driver (ten 100-MLP subprocess chunks). Paired per-MLP final_layer_mse and adjusted_final_layer_score by mlp_name against B26's champion Full report (B25/S3 submitted baseline).

vs_b46_champion.note: GS Full MSE (6.0794e-06) is essentially identical to B46 (6.0785e-06); GS is +4% higher on ADJUSTED score (its extra Gram-Schmidt ops cost ~+4% multiplier). B46 is locally slightly better but UNGRADEABLE (fnp.linalg.qr); GS is the best GRADEABLE option.

verdict: PASS. The GS candidate has a COMPLETE Full gate and satisfies all AGENTS.md step-7 GATE prerequisites: paired Full CI entirely below zero (MSE -5.40 sigma, adjusted -5.16 sigma) and -20.1% over last_submitted_score (>> 5% bar), zero failure flags. It matches B46's Full MSE exactly, at +4% multiplier, using ONLY gradeable ops (no fnp.linalg.qr).
next_action: GS is fully submission-ready. Submitting it is a FRESH lead/user decision (not covered by the B47/branch-1 B46 authorization; S4 was just consumed) and doubles as the decisive qr-hypothesis test: if it GRADES, it realizes a ~-20% improvement over last_submitted / ~-17% over the S3 leaderboard 6.6845e-07 and confirms qr was the S4 cause; if it also fails, the grader issue is not qr. Suggested: promote GS over the ungradeable B46 (GS is the submittable champion) and submit per step 7. I did NOT submit or promote -- infra/gate only.
full_submission_gate: PASS (gate prerequisites met; actual network submission pending lead/user).
